name: Charlie Chaplin

acts.description: Serial relationships with teenage girls; married Mildred Harris at 16, Lita Grey at 16, both when he was in his 30s

behavior_summary: The most famous filmmaker of the silent era had a persistent pattern of pursuing and marrying teenage girls while he was in his 30s. His fourth wife Oona O'Neill was 18 when he was 54.

art.summary: Invented screen comedy. The Tramp is one of the most recognizable fictional characters in history. The Great Dictator was a courageous anti-fascist statement.

verdict.blurb: City Lights is one of the most emotionally devastating films ever made. The man who made it had a documented preference for teenage girls that spanned thirty years and four marriages. The Tramp is timeless. The pattern was not incidental.
